title: Design seams for testing and substitution
description: Introduce explicit boundaries where external dependencies can be substituted, so units are testable without real infrastructure.
body: Use when a module is hard to test because it directly reaches network, clock, filesystem, or other systems. Do not bury external calls deep inside business logic with no substitution point. Actions: define an interface at the boundary, inject the dependency, and keep the core logic pure and deterministic so tests use a fake or in-memory implementation. Validation signals include unit tests running with no real network/disk and a clear seam in the dependency graph. Risk boundary: over-abstracting every call adds indirection cost; add a seam where substitution is actually needed for testing or change, not everywhere.
